title: assemblykor - Handover (2026-03-07)
text: 13 commits single day. Key milestones: Initial release v0.1.0 (speeches, 6 tutorials, CSV, helpers) member_id linking seminars/speeches Speeches expanded 37 committees, 16-22대 Speeches scoped back 22대 과방위 (package size) 국정감사 speeches added Votes + roll_calls + learnr interactive tutorials added %>% pipe standardization + startup message set_ko_font() helper Custom CSS theme (Catppuccin Mocha dark) learnr CSS injection fix: htmltools::includeCSS() inline embedding

title: assemblykor - Handover (2026-03-07)
text: CSS rendering fix: learnr tutorials used relative CSS path (../css/) failed Shiny’s restricted file serving. Fixed switching htmltools::includeCSS(system.file(...)) inline embedding. 6 tutorials patched (commit cae933c). n_bills clarification: Tutorial 1 now explains n_bills sum lead-proposed co-sponsored bills, just lead proposals. Added context data introduction (Section 1) mutate example (Section 4).

title: assemblykor - Handover (2026-03-07)
text: inst/tutorials/01-tidyverse-basics/01-tidyverse-basics.Rmd - n_bills 설명 추가 inst/tutorials/css/assemblykor.css - (확인 필요)

title: assemblykor - Handover (2026-03-07)
text: CSS theming: Custom dark theme (652 lines), Catppuccin Mocha inspired. Fonts: Inter (UI), JetBrains Mono (code). Uses htmltools::includeCSS() tutorial Rmd, YAML css: parameter. Data pipeline: data-raw/prepare_data.R (R) + process_*.py (Python). Raw source files (.gitignored): speeches_all_committees.csv (3.1GB), speeches_full.parquet (1.4GB), roll_calls_raw.csv (44MB). Dependencies: arrow required download functions. Core package depends base R + utils. LazyData: Compressed xz. Package install includes 7 .rda files.

title: assemblykor - Handover (2026-03-07)
text: n_bills legislators: includes co-sponsorship. Median 702, max 4,198. Can surprise students expecting lead-proposed bills. n_bills_lead: lead proposals . Median 56, max 696. 661 unique legislators across 947 rows (serve multiple assemblies). wealth unit: thousands KRW (divide 1,000,000 billions). roll_calls API available 22대.

title: assemblykor - Handover (2026-03-11)
text: get_bill_texts() - Download bill rationale texts (60,925, ~40MB parquet) get_proposers() - Download co-sponsorship records (769,773, ~25MB parquet) list_tutorials() - List available tutorials run_tutorial(name) - Run learnr interactive tutorial open_tutorial(name) - Copy Rmd working directory path_to_file(file) - Get CSV file path set_ko_font() - Auto-set Korean font ggplot2

title: assemblykor - Handover (2026-03-11)
text: Two formats: - inst/tutorials/ - learnr interactive (browser-based, English section headings) - inst/rmd-tutorials/ - plain Rmd (RStudio editing, Korean headings)

title: assemblykor - Handover (2026-03-11)
text: Created 3 new learnr tutorials: 07-roll-call-analysis: Covers votes/roll_calls data, Rice Index calculation, rebel vote identification, voting heatmap, agreement matrix. exercise includes full data prep learnr isolation. 3 exercises hints. 08-bill-success: Binary outcome creation, OLS vs logistic comparison, glm(family=binomial), odds ratios, predicted probabilities, AIC/pseudo-R2 comparison. Quiz blocks binary variables logistic concepts. 3 exercises. 09-speech-patterns: Role analysis, speech frequency/length, turn structure, agenda keyword dictionaries (AI, Semiconductor, Broadcasting, Telecom, Privacy), member-agenda heatmap, diversity measurement. 3 exercises.

title: assemblykor - Handover (2026-03-11)
text: 6 existing learnr tutorials fully rewritten match enhanced rmd-tutorial content: 01: Added variable explanation table, comparison operators table, detailed pipe explanation, arrange section, bills/wealth data exploration, join types table. 5 exercises. 02: Added density comparison (Gangnam property), chart selection guide, polishing step--step section party colors, ggplot2 grammar explanation, quiz position=“fill”. 4 exercises. 03: Added OLS formula explanation, coefficient plot broom::tidy(), model diagnostics table, log transformation explanation, results summary table. Pre-built merged dataset. 4 exercises. 04: Added detailed /within explanation, OVB concept, demeaning explanation, switchers concept, time-invariant limitation, cluster SE. Uses fixest::feols() etable(). 3 exercises. 05: Added speeches data analysis (role-based TF-IDF), AI keyword tracking speeches, temporal comparison (20th vs 22nd), expanded stopwords (46 terms), preprocessing flow diagram, tokenization limitation note. 3 exercises. 06: Added network concept visual (5-node example), centrality comparison scatter plot, cross-party heatmap, detailed modularity explanation, 21st vs 22nd assembly comparison exercise. 3 exercises hints.

title: assemblykor - Handover (2026-03-11)
text: 9 tutorials now follow pattern: - English section headings (Korean data content preserved) - set_ko_font() instead manual font detection code - CSS injection via htmltools::includeCSS(system.file(...)) - tutorial_options(exercise.eval = FALSE) - exercise chunk self-contained (includes data prep) - Korean Unicode escapes string literals learnr context

title: assemblykor - Handover (2026-03-21)
text: CRAN pre-test rejection 대응 및 재제출 완료.

title: assemblykor - Handover (2026-03-21)
text: CRAN 자동 검사에서 1 ERROR, 1 WARNING, 2 NOTEs로 reject됨. 원인과 수정 내역: 원인: Rd 파일 내 한글(CJK) 문자를 pdflatex이 처리 못함 \enc{}{} 매크로 시도했으나 UTF-8 로케일에서 항상 한글(1번 인자) 선택하여 무효 최종 해결: Rd 파일에서 한글 전부 제거, 영문 설명으로 대체 수정 파일: R/data.R, R/download.R, R/utils.R + 재생성된 man/*.Rd 14개 원인: (20th-22nd)에서 스펠체커가 “th”, “nd”를 독립 단어로 인식 해결: (assemblies 20 22, 2016-2026)으로 변경 원인: \url{https://open.assembly.go.kr} 루트가 봇 요청 거부 해결: \url{} 제거, “Open National Assembly Information API (Republic Korea)”로 대체 README.md도 auto-link(<https://...>) 제거

title: assemblykor - Handover (2026-03-21)
text: R CMD check ---cran: 0 ERROR, 0 WARNING, 4 NOTEs (모두 무해) cran-comments.md 재제출 사유 추가 GitHub push 완료, CRAN 웹폼 재제출 완료 (2026-03-21)

title: assemblykor - Handover (2026-03-21)
text: \enc{Korean}{ASCII} 매크로는 CJK 문자에 사실상 무효 (UTF-8 로케일이 기본인 현대 시스템에서 항상 1번 인자 선택) CRAN PDF 매뉴얼은 pdflatex 사용하므로 Rd 파일에 한글 포함 불가 한글 데이터 값 설명은 HTML docs(pkgdown)와 vignettes에서만 가능

title: Data Codebook
text: 947 rows, 15 variables. MP metadata 20th-22nd Korean National Assembly. Unit observation: legislator-assembly Key: member_id + assembly (unique) Source: Open National Assembly API

title: Data Codebook
text: 60,925 rows, 9 variables. Legislative bill metadata (20th-22nd assembly). Unit observation: bill Key: bill_id (unique) Join: proposer_id links legislators$member_id Source: Open National Assembly API

title: Data Codebook
text: 2,928 rows, 14 variables. Legislator asset declaration panel (2015-2025, 13 disclosure periods). Unit observation: legislator-year Key: member_id + year (unique) Units: monetary values thousands KRW (1 unit = 1,000 won) Source: OpenWatch (CC -SA 4.0)

title: Data Codebook
text: 5,962 rows, 18 variables. Legislator-year policy seminar activity (17th-22nd assembly, 2000-2025). Unit observation: legislator-year Key: member_id + year (note: ~5% member_id NA) Source: National Assembly Seminar Database

title: Data Codebook
text: 15,843 rows, 9 variables. Committee speech records Science ICT Committee (22nd assembly, 2024). Unit observation: speech turn Key: date + speech_order (unique within meeting) Source: National Assembly committee minutes

title: Data Codebook
text: 8,050 rows, 13 variables. Plenary vote tallies (20th-22nd assembly). Unit observation: bill vote Key: bill_id (unique) Join: bill_id links bills$bill_id (~40% match rate; votes includes committee alternatives budget bills bills) Source: Open National Assembly API

title: Data Codebook
text: 383,739 rows, 8 variables. Member-level roll call votes (22nd assembly, 1,286 bills). Unit observation: legislator-bill vote Key: member_id + bill_id (unique) Join: member_id links legislators$member_id; bill_id links votes$bill_id Source: Open National Assembly API

title: Data Codebook
text: datasets share member_id primary join key. Use assembly secondary key joining datasets span multiple assembly terms.
code: legislators                    (member_id + assembly)                    /       |        \                   /        |         \                wealth   seminars   bills             (member_id) (member_id) (proposer_id)                                       |                                     votes                                   (bill_id)                                       |                                   roll_calls                                (bill_id + member_id)                                       |                                   legislators                                   (member_id)      speeches --- legislators (member_id, 22nd assembly only)

title: Getting Started with assemblykor
text: assemblykor provides seven built-datasets Korean National Assembly teaching quantitative methods political science: legislators: 947 MP records (20th-22nd assemblies) bills: 60,925 legislative bills wealth: 2,928 legislator-year asset declarations seminars: 5,962 legislator-year seminar activity records speeches: 15,843 committee speech records (22nd, Science & ICT) votes: 8,050 plenary vote tallies (20th-22nd) roll_calls: 383,739 member-level roll call votes (22nd)
code: library(assemblykor) #>  #>   ┌───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐ #>   │ assemblykor 0.1.2                                             │ #>   │ Korean National Assembly Data for Political Science Education │ #>   └───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘ #>  #>   7 built-in datasets: #>     legislators    947 recs   MPs (20-22nd) #>     bills       60,925 recs   Bills proposed #>     wealth       2,928 recs   Asset declarations #>     seminars     5,962 recs   Policy seminars #>     speeches    15,843 recs   Committee speeches (22nd, Sci & ICT) #>     votes        8,050 recs   Plenary vote tallies #>     roll_calls 383,739 recs   Member-level votes (22nd) #>  #>   Downloadable: #>     get_bill_texts()           Bill propose-reason texts #>     get_proposers()            Co-sponsorship records #>  #>   Tutorials: #>     list_tutorials()           See all 9 tutorials #>     run_tutorial(1)            Launch in browser (interactive) #>     open_tutorial(1)           Copy Rmd to working directory #>  #>   Korean font for ggplot2:     set_ko_font() #>  #>   https://CRAN.R-project.org/package=assemblykor

title: Getting Started with assemblykor
code: data(legislators) str(legislators) #> 'data.frame':    947 obs. of  15 variables: #>  $ member_id    : chr  "XQ98168F" "60490713" "IH436704" "8I61593E" ... #>  $ assembly     : int  20 20 20 20 20 20 20 20 20 20 ... #>  $ name         : chr  "강길부" "강병원" "강석진" "강석호" ... #>  $ name_hanja   : chr  "姜吉夫" "姜炳遠" "姜錫振" "姜碩鎬" ... #>  $ name_eng     : chr  "KANG GHILBOO" "KANG BYUNGWON" "KANG SEOGJIN" "KANG SEOKHO" ... #>  $ party        : chr  "무소속" "더불어민주당" "자유한국당" "자유한국당" ... #>  $ party_elected: chr  "무소속" "더불어민주당" "새누리당" "새누리당" ... #>  $ district     : chr  "울산 울주군" "서울 은평구을" "경남 산청군함양군거창군합천군" "경북 영양군영덕군봉화군울진군" ... #>  $ district_type: chr  "constituency" "constituency" "constituency" "constituency" ... #>  $ committees   : chr  "산업통상자원중소벤처기업위원회, 4차 산업혁명 특별위원회, 예산결산특별위원회, 교육문화체육관광위원회" "" "농림축산식품해양수산위원회, 국회운영위원회, 보건복지위원회, 예산결산특별위원회" "농림축산식품해양수산위원회, 외교통일위원회, 정보위원회, 정치개혁 특별위원회, 행정안전위원회, 안전행정위원회" ... #>  $ gender       : chr  "M" "M" "M" "M" ... #>  $ birth_date   : Date, format: "1942-06-05" "1971-07-09" ... #>  $ seniority    : int  4 2 1 3 4 1 3 2 2 1 ... #>  $ n_bills      : int  312 1078 694 583 1131 393 1478 932 1284 1054 ... #>  $ n_bills_lead : int  21 94 53 43 89 74 75 55 78 64 ...

title: Getting Started with assemblykor
code: gender_tab <- table(legislators$assembly, legislators$gender) gender_tab #>      #>        F   M #>   20  53 267 #>   21  64 258 #>   22  64 241 prop.table(gender_tab, margin = 1) #>      #>              F         M #>   20 0.1656250 0.8343750 #>   21 0.1987578 0.8012422 #>   22 0.2098361 0.7901639

title: Getting Started with assemblykor
text: Senior legislators produce bills, high variance.
code: boxplot(n_bills_lead ~ seniority, data = legislators,         xlab = "Terms served", ylab = "Bills proposed (as lead)",         main = "Seniority and Legislative Productivity",         col = "lightblue")

title: Getting Started with assemblykor
text: bills expire end assembly term (임기만료폐기). small fraction pass original form (원안가결).
code: data(bills)  # Top 5 outcomes outcome_counts <- sort(table(bills$result), decreasing = TRUE) barplot(outcome_counts[1:5], las = 2, col = "steelblue",         main = "Most Common Bill Outcomes")

title: Getting Started with assemblykor
code: bills$month <- format(bills$propose_date, "%Y-%m") monthly <- aggregate(bill_id ~ month, data = bills, FUN = length) names(monthly) <- c("month", "count") monthly <- monthly[order(monthly$month), ]  plot(seq_len(nrow(monthly)), monthly$count, type = "l",      xlab = "Month (index)", ylab = "Bills proposed",      main = "Monthly Bill Proposals (20th-22nd Assembly)")

title: Getting Started with assemblykor
text: wealth dataset legislator-year panel ideal practicing fixed-effects regression.
code: data(wealth)  # Distribution of net worth hist(wealth$net_worth / 1e6, breaks = 50, col = "coral",      main = "Legislator Net Worth Distribution",      xlab = "Net Worth (billion KRW)")

title: Getting Started with assemblykor
text: Korean legislators hold large share wealth real estate, reflecting broader patterns Korean household wealth.
code: wealth$re_share <- ifelse(wealth$total_assets > 0,                           wealth$real_estate / wealth$total_assets, NA)  boxplot(re_share ~ year, data = wealth,         xlab = "Year", ylab = "Real estate / total assets",         main = "Real Estate as Share of Legislator Wealth",         col = "lightyellow")

title: Getting Started with assemblykor
text: Governing-party legislators tend lower cross-party collaboration policy seminars, pattern consistent “closing ranks” hypothesis.
code: data(seminars)  # Governing vs opposition party gov_means <- tapply(seminars$cross_party_ratio,                     seminars$is_governing, mean, na.rm = TRUE) barplot(gov_means, names.arg = c("Opposition", "Governing"),         ylab = "Cross-party ratio", col = c("dodgerblue", "tomato"),         main = "Cross-Party Seminar Collaboration")

title: Getting Started with assemblykor
text: datasets share member_id /assembly columns:
code: library(dplyr)  # Merge legislators with wealth leg_wealth <- legislators %>%   inner_join(wealth, by = "member_id", relationship = "many-to-many")  # Productivity vs wealth leg_wealth %>%   group_by(district_type) %>%   summarise(     n = n(),     median_net_worth = median(net_worth / 1e6, na.rm = TRUE),     median_bills = median(n_bills_lead, na.rm = TRUE)   ) #> # A tibble: 2 × 4 #>   district_type     n median_net_worth median_bills #>   <chr>         <int>            <dbl>        <dbl> #> 1 constituency   4489             1.48           62 #> 2 proportional    466             1.23           65

title: Getting Started with assemblykor
text: bills pass near-unanimous support. left tail reveals contested legislation party discipline breaks .
code: data(votes)  # Yes-vote share distribution votes$yes_rate <- votes$yes / votes$voted hist(votes$yes_rate, breaks = 40, col = "lightgreen",      main = "Distribution of Yes-Vote Share",      xlab = "Proportion yes")

title: Getting Started with assemblykor
code: data(roll_calls) library(dplyr)  # Party discipline: how often do members vote with their party majority? party_votes <- roll_calls %>%   group_by(bill_id, party) %>%   mutate(party_majority = names(which.max(table(vote)))) %>%   ungroup() %>%   mutate(with_party = vote == party_majority)  party_votes %>%   group_by(party) %>%   summarise(     n_members = n_distinct(member_id),     discipline = mean(with_party, na.rm = TRUE)   ) %>%   filter(n_members >= 5) %>%   arrange(desc(discipline)) #> # A tibble: 4 × 3 #>   party        n_members discipline #>   <chr>            <int>      <dbl> #> 1 조국혁신당          13      0.888 #> 2 더불어민주당       169      0.856 #> 3 국민의힘           107      0.762 #> 4 무소속               6      0.753

title: Getting Started with assemblykor
code: data(speeches)  # Who speaks most in committee? leg_speeches <- speeches[speeches$role == "legislator", ] speaker_counts <- sort(table(leg_speeches$speaker_name), decreasing = TRUE) barplot(speaker_counts[1:10], las = 2, col = "plum",         main = "Top 10 Most Active Speakers (Sci & ICT Committee)")

title: Getting Started with assemblykor
text: text analysis, download bill propose-reason texts: network analysis, download full co-sponsorship records: See vignette("codebook") full data dictionary, ?get_bill_texts ?get_proposers download function details.
code: texts <- get_bill_texts() proposers <- get_proposers()

title: 5-Minute Quickstart
code: install.packages("assemblykor") library(assemblykor) #>  #>   ┌───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐ #>   │ assemblykor 0.1.2                                             │ #>   │ Korean National Assembly Data for Political Science Education │ #>   └───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘ #>  #>   7 built-in datasets: #>     legislators    947 recs   MPs (20-22nd) #>     bills       60,925 recs   Bills proposed #>     wealth       2,928 recs   Asset declarations #>     seminars     5,962 recs   Policy seminars #>     speeches    15,843 recs   Committee speeches (22nd, Sci & ICT) #>     votes        8,050 recs   Plenary vote tallies #>     roll_calls 383,739 recs   Member-level votes (22nd) #>  #>   Downloadable: #>     get_bill_texts()           Bill propose-reason texts #>     get_proposers()            Co-sponsorship records #>  #>   Tutorials: #>     list_tutorials()           See all 9 tutorials #>     run_tutorial(1)            Launch in browser (interactive) #>     open_tutorial(1)           Copy Rmd to working directory #>  #>   Korean font for ggplot2:     set_ko_font() #>  #>   https://CRAN.R-project.org/package=assemblykor

title: 5-Minute Quickstart
code: # All datasets load lazily - just call their name head(legislators, 3) #>   member_id assembly   name name_hanja      name_eng        party party_elected #> 1  XQ98168F       20 강길부     姜吉夫  KANG GHILBOO       무소속        무소속 #> 2  60490713       20 강병원     姜炳遠 KANG BYUNGWON 더불어민주당  더불어민주당 #> 3  IH436704       20 강석진     姜錫振  KANG SEOGJIN   자유한국당      새누리당 #>                        district district_type #> 1                   울산 울주군  constituency #> 2                 서울 은평구을  constituency #> 3 경남 산청군함양군거창군합천군  constituency #>                                                                                            committees #> 1 산업통상자원중소벤처기업위원회, 4차 산업혁명 특별위원회, 예산결산특별위원회, 교육문화체육관광위원회 #> 2 what: Quick analysis: gender and legislative productivity
title: 5-Minute Quickstart
code: library(dplyr)  legislators %>%   filter(assembly == 22) %>%   group_by(gender) %>%   summarise(     n = n(),     median_bills_led = median(n_bills_lead, na.rm = TRUE)   ) #> # A tibble: 2 × 3 #>   gender     n median_bills_led #>   <chr>  <int>            <dbl> #> 1 F         64               54 #> 2 M        241               44

title: 5-Minute Quickstart
text: 5% bills pass original form.
code: bills %>%   count(result, sort = TRUE) %>%   head(5) #>         result     n #> 1 임기만료폐기 30678 #> 2 대안반영폐기 13692 #> 3         <NA> 12171 #> 4     수정가결  2222 #> 5     원안가결  1048

title: 5-Minute Quickstart
code: # Net worth in billion KRW hist(wealth$net_worth / 1e6, breaks = 50, col = "steelblue",      main = "Legislator Net Worth", xlab = "Billion KRW")

title: 5-Minute Quickstart
text: bills pass near-unanimously; handful fiercely contested.
code: votes$yes_rate <- votes$yes / votes$voted hist(votes$yes_rate, breaks = 40, col = "coral",      main = "Distribution of Yes-Vote Share", xlab = "Proportion yes")

title: 5-Minute Quickstart
text: datasets share member_id easy merging:
code: # Do wealthier legislators propose more bills? leg_wealth <- legislators %>%   filter(assembly == 22) %>%   inner_join(wealth %>% filter(year == 2024),              by = "member_id")  cor(leg_wealth$n_bills_lead, leg_wealth$net_worth,     use = "complete.obs") #> [1] -0.05360641

title: 5-Minute Quickstart
text: Nine Korean-language tutorials cover full methods sequence:
code: list_tutorials()           # See all 9 run_tutorial(1)            # Interactive in browser open_tutorial(1)           # Copy Rmd to your directory

title: 5-Minute Quickstart
text: vignette("introduction") deeper examples vignette("codebook") full data dictionary ?legislators, ?bills, etc. variable-level documentation

title: Authors and Citation
text: Kyusik Yang. Author, maintainer.

title: Authors and Citation
text: Yang K (2026). assemblykor: Korean National Assembly Data Political Science Education. R package version 0.1.2, https://CRAN.R-project.org/package=assemblykor.
code: @Manual{,   title = {assemblykor: Korean National Assembly Data for Political Science Education},   author = {Kyusik Yang},   year = {2026},   url = {https://CRAN.R-project.org/package=assemblykor},   note = {R package version 0.1.2}, }

title: assemblykor: Korean National Assembly Data
text: Korean National Assembly data political science education. Documentation: https://kyusik-yang.github.io/assemblykor/

title: assemblykor: Korean National Assembly Data
text: goal assemblykor provide curated collection Korean National Assembly datasets teaching quantitative methods political science. Think Korean politics counterpart palmerpenguins. package includes seven built-datasets covering legislators, bills, asset declarations, policy seminars, committee speeches, plenary votes, roll call records, drawn public data Korean National Assembly (2000-2026).

title: assemblykor: Korean National Assembly Data
text: package tutorials follow tidyverse conventions. use dplyr, ggplot2, tidyr, pipe operator (%>%) throughout. tidyverse-first teaching? Readability: tidyverse syntax reads like sequence data operations, reducing cognitive load beginners (Cetinkaya-Rundel et al., 2022). Consistency: Packages share common grammar data structure, learning one makes next easier (Wickham et al., 2019). things quickly: Students can perform meaningful data analysis day one, rather spending weeks base R syntax (Robinson, 2017). Community standard: modern R textbooks, including R Data Science, adopt tidyverse default, making easier students find help resources. Note: package designed educational purposes . datasets processed curated classroom use may reflect --date complete records. research policy analysis, please verify original data sources listed Data sources section .

title: assemblykor: Korean National Assembly Data
text: legislators 947 records 20-22대 국회의원 메타데이터 (이름, 정당, 선거구, 성별, 선수, 발의 건수) bills 60,925 records 법안 메타데이터 (제목, 위원회, 발의일, 처리 결과, 대표발의자) wealth 2,928 records 의원 재산신고 패널 (순자산, 부동산, 예금, 주식, 13개 시점) seminars 5,962 records 정책세미나 활동 패널 (개최 건수, 초당적 비율, 여당 여부) speeches 15,843 records 22대 과학기술정보방송통신위원회 상임위 회의록 전수 (발언 전문, 화자 역할 포함) votes 8,050 records 본회의 표결 결과 (20-22대, 찬성/반대/기권 수, 의결 결과) roll_calls 383,739 records 22대 개별 의원 표결 기록 (의원별 찬성/반대/기권/불참, 1,286개 법안)

title: assemblykor: Korean National Assembly Data
text: ggplot2 plots Korean text (axis labels, titles) may show broken characters. Run per session fix : auto-detects Korean font system (macOS, Windows, Linux). interactive tutorials (run_tutorial()) handle automatically.
code: set_ko_font() #> Korean font set: Apple SD Gothic Neo

title: assemblykor: Korean National Assembly Data
text: Larger datasets available via download functions (requires arrow package):
code: # Bill propose-reason texts (60,925 texts, ~25 MB download) texts <- get_bill_texts()  # Co-sponsorship records (769,773 rows, ~6 MB download) proposers <- get_proposers()

title: assemblykor: Korean National Assembly Data
text: package includes nine Korean-language tutorials designed classroom use political science methods courses: tutorial available two formats: Option : Interactive browser (recommended self-study) Requires learnr package (install.packages("learnr")). Students can type run code directly browser hints solutions. Option B: Plain R Markdown (editing RStudio) Students can edit knit Rmd file RStudio pace.
code: # Launch an interactive tutorial with exercises in the browser run_tutorial(1)  # or run_tutorial("01-tidyverse-basics") # List available tutorials list_tutorials()  # Copy an Rmd file to your working directory open_tutorial(1)  # or open_tutorial("01-tidyverse-basics")

title: assemblykor: Korean National Assembly Data
text: datasets share member_id /assembly columns easy joining:
code: # Merge legislators with wealth data leg_wealth <- legislators %>%   inner_join(wealth, by = "member_id", relationship = "many-to-many")

title: assemblykor: Korean National Assembly Data
text: CSV versions smaller datasets available teaching file /O:
code: # Find the file path path_to_file("legislators.csv")  # Read directly legislators_csv <- read.csv(path_to_file("legislators.csv"), fileEncoding = "UTF-8")

title: assemblykor: Korean National Assembly Data
text: data package derived publicly available sources. Inspired : palmerpenguins (Horst, Hill & Gorman, 2020), demonstrates domain-specific datasets can make methods teaching engaging.

title: assemblykor: Korean National Assembly Data
text: package intended educational use (teaching quantitative methods political science courses). datasets processed, filtered, cases sampled classroom convenience. treated authoritative records. research policy analysis, please consult original data sources listed verify data independently.

code: citation("assemblykor") Yang, Kyusik (2026). assemblykor: Korean National Assembly Data for Political Science Education. R package version 0.1.2. https://CRAN.R-project.org/package=assemblykor

what: assemblykor: Korean National Assembly Data for Political Science Education — assemblykor-package
title: assemblykor: Korean National Assembly Data for Political Science Education — assemblykor-package
text: Provides ready--use datasets Korean National Assembly teaching quantitative methods political science. Includes five built-datasets covering legislator metadata, bills, asset declarations, policy seminars, committee speeches.

title: assemblykor: Korean National Assembly Data for Political Science Education — assemblykor-package
text: legislators: 947 MP records (20th-22nd assemblies) bills: 60,925 legislative bills wealth: 2,928 legislator-year asset declarations seminars: 5,962 legislator-year seminar records speeches: 15,843 speech records (22nd, Science & ICT Committee) votes: 8,050 plenary vote tallies (20th-22nd assemblies) roll_calls: 383,739 member-level roll call votes (22nd assembly)

title: assemblykor: Korean National Assembly Data for Political Science Education — assemblykor-package
text: get_bill_texts: 60,925 bill propose-reason texts get_proposers: 769,773 co-sponsorship records

title: assemblykor: Korean National Assembly Data for Political Science Education — assemblykor-package
text: Nine Korean-language tutorials covering tidyverse, visualization, regression, panel data, text analysis, network analysis, roll call analysis, bill success, speech patterns. Use list_tutorials see tutorials, open_tutorial copy working directory.

title: assemblykor: Korean National Assembly Data for Political Science Education — assemblykor-package
text: Maintainer: Kyusik Yang kyusik.yang@nyu.edu

what: Bills Proposed in the Korean National Assembly (20th-22nd) — bills
title: Bills Proposed in the Korean National Assembly (20th-22nd) — bills
text: Metadata 60,925 legislative bills proposed 20th 22nd Korean National Assembly (2016-2026).

title: Bills Proposed in the Korean National Assembly (20th-22nd) — bills
code: bills

title: Bills Proposed in the Korean National Assembly (20th-22nd) — bills
text: data frame 60,925 rows 9 variables: bill_id Unique bill identifier National Assembly system bill_no Numeric bill number assembly Assembly number (20, 21, 22) bill_name Full bill title Korean committee Standing committee bill referred propose_date Date bill formally proposed result Legislative outcome Korean. Common values include passed -, expired term end, incorporated alternative bill. See table(bills$result) values. proposer Name lead (primary) proposer proposer_id MONA_CD lead proposer (links legislators$member_id)

title: Bills Proposed in the Korean National Assembly (20th-22nd) — bills
text: Open National Assembly Information API (Republic Korea).

title: Bills Proposed in the Korean National Assembly (20th-22nd) — bills
text: Korean National Assembly seen dramatic increase bill proposals: 21st Assembly produced 23,655 bills versus 21,594 20th. bills expire end assembly term (term expiry); 5\ Use get_bill_texts() download full propose-reason texts text analysis, get_proposers() complete co-sponsorship records (769,773 rows).

title: Bills Proposed in the Korean National Assembly (20th-22nd) — bills

title: Download bill propose-reason texts — get_bill_texts
text: Downloads full propose-reason texts (jean-iyu) 60,925 bills. file approximately 25 MB cached locally first download. Requires arrow package read parquet files.

title: Download bill propose-reason texts — get_bill_texts
code: get_bill_texts(cache_dir = NULL, force_download = FALSE)

title: Download bill propose-reason texts — get_bill_texts
text: cache_dir Directory cache downloaded files. Defaults tools::R_user_dir("assemblykor", "cache"). force_download Logical. TRUE, re-download even cached.

title: Download bill propose-reason texts — get_bill_texts
text: data frame 60,925 rows 3 variables: bill_id Bill identifier (links bills$bill_id) propose_reason Full text propose-reason statement (Korean) scrape_status Data collection status: "ok", "empty", "no_csrf", "error"

title: Download bill propose-reason texts — get_bill_texts
code: # \donttest{ if (requireNamespace("arrow", quietly = TRUE)) {   texts <- get_bill_texts(cache_dir = tempdir())   nchar_dist <- nchar(texts$propose_reason)   hist(nchar_dist, breaks = 100, main = "Length of Propose-Reason Texts") } #> Downloading bill texts (~25 MB)... #> Cached at: /tmp/RtmphB8v02/bill_texts.parquet  # }

title: Download bill co-sponsorship records — get_proposers
text: Downloads complete proposer records (769,773 rows) listing every legislator co-sponsored bill. Requires arrow package.

title: Download bill co-sponsorship records — get_proposers
code: get_proposers(cache_dir = NULL, force_download = FALSE)

title: Download bill co-sponsorship records — get_proposers
text: cache_dir Directory cache downloaded files. Defaults tools::R_user_dir("assemblykor", "cache"). force_download Logical. TRUE, re-download even cached.

title: Download bill co-sponsorship records — get_proposers
text: data frame 769,773 rows 8 variables: bill_id Bill identifier (links bills$bill_id) bill_no Numeric bill number bill_name Bill title Korean propose_date Proposal date proposer_name Legislator name proposer_party Party affiliation time co-sponsorship member_id Legislator identifier (links legislators$member_id) is_lead Logical: TRUE lead (primary) proposer, FALSE co-sponsor

title: Download bill co-sponsorship records — get_proposers
code: # \donttest{ if (requireNamespace("arrow", quietly = TRUE) &&     requireNamespace("dplyr", quietly = TRUE)) {   props <- get_proposers(cache_dir = tempdir())    # Build co-sponsorship edgelist   leads <- dplyr::select(     dplyr::filter(props, is_lead), bill_id, lead = member_id   )   cosponsors <- dplyr::select(     dplyr::filter(props, !is_lead), bill_id, cosponsor = member_id   )   edges <- dplyr::inner_join(     leads, cosponsors,     by = "bill_id", relationship = "many-to-many"   ) } #> Downloading proposer records (~6 MB)... #> Cached at: /tmp/RtmphB8v02/proposers.parquet # }

what: Members of the Korean National Assembly (20th-22nd) — legislators
title: Members of the Korean National Assembly (20th-22nd) — legislators
text: Biographical political metadata 947 records legislators served 20th (2016-2020), 21st (2020-2024), 22nd (2024-2028) Korean National Assembly. legislators appear multiple assemblies.

title: Members of the Korean National Assembly (20th-22nd) — legislators
code: legislators

title: Members of the Korean National Assembly (20th-22nd) — legislators
text: data frame 947 rows 15 variables: member_id Unique legislator identifier (MONA_CD National Assembly API) assembly Assembly number (20, 21, 22) name Name Korean (hangul) name_hanja Name Chinese characters (hanja) name_eng Name English (romanized) party Party affiliation assembly term party_elected Party time election district Electoral district name, party list position proportional members district_type Election type: "constituency" "proportional" committees Standing committee assignments (comma-separated) gender "M" (male) "F" (female) birth_date Date birth seniority Number terms served, including current (1 = first-term) n_bills Total bills participated (lead proposer co-sponsor) n_bills_lead Bills proposed lead (primary) proposer

title: Members of the Korean National Assembly (20th-22nd) — legislators
text: Open National Assembly Information API (Republic Korea). License: public domain (Korean government open data).

title: Members of the Korean National Assembly (20th-22nd) — legislators
text: 661 unique legislators served across three assemblies. member_id consistent across assemblies, legislators can tracked time. Party names may differ party (mid-term) party_elected (election day) due party mergers name changes, common Korean politics.

title: Members of the Korean National Assembly (20th-22nd) — legislators
code: data(legislators)  # Party composition by assembly table(legislators$assembly, legislators$party) #>      #>      개혁신당 국민의당 국민의힘 기본소득당 녹색정의당 더불어민주당 무소속 #>   20        0       25        0          0          0          138      6 #>   21        0        1      123          1          2          180      6 #>   22        3        0      109          1          0          171      3 #>      #>      민주평화당 바른미래당 사회민주당 새누리당 시대전환 열린민주당 자유한국당 #>   20          6         17          0       10        0          0        111 #>   21          0          0          0        0        1          1          0 #>   22          0          0          1        0        0          0          0 #>      #>      정의당 조국혁신당 진보당 #>   20      7          0      0 #>   21      6          0      1 #>   22      0         13      4  # Gender gap in bill production tapply(legislators$n_bills_lead, legislators$gender, median) #>  F  M  #> 63 55   # First-term vs senior legislators boxplot(n_bills_lead ~ seniority, data = legislators,         xlab = "Terms served", ylab = "Bills proposed (lead)")

title: List available tutorials — list_tutorials
text: Lists tutorial R Markdown files included package. Tutorials designed classroom use Korean political science methods courses. tutorial available two formats: Plain Rmd editing RStudio (open_tutorial) Interactive learnr format (run_tutorial)

title: List available tutorials — list_tutorials
code: list_tutorials()

title: List available tutorials — list_tutorials
text: character vector tutorial file names (invisibly).

title: List available tutorials — list_tutorials
code: list_tutorials() #> Available tutorials: #>  #>    01-tidyverse-basics.Rmd  #>    02-data-visualization.Rmd  #>    03-regression.Rmd  #>    04-panel-data.Rmd  #>    05-text-analysis.Rmd  #>    06-network-analysis.Rmd  #>    07-roll-call-analysis.Rmd  #>    08-bill-success.Rmd  #>    09-speech-patterns.Rmd  #>  #> Usage: #>   open_tutorial(1)       # Copy Rmd to working directory #>   run_tutorial(1)        # Launch interactive version in browser

title: Open a tutorial file — open_tutorial
text: Copies tutorial R Markdown file specified directory (default: current working directory) students can edit run RStudio.

title: Open a tutorial file — open_tutorial
code: open_tutorial(name, dest_dir = getwd())

title: Open a tutorial file — open_tutorial
text: name Tutorial name (without .Rmd extension), number corresponding tutorial order (1-9). dest_dir Directory copy file . Defaults current working directory.

title: Open a tutorial file — open_tutorial
text: path copied file (invisibly).

title: Open a tutorial file — open_tutorial
code: if (interactive()) {   # Copy by name   open_tutorial("01-tidyverse-basics")    # Copy by number   open_tutorial(1) }

what: Path to assemblykor CSV files — path_to_file

code: path_to_file(file = NULL)

constituency #> 4      새누리당 경북 영양군영덕군봉화군울진군  constituency #> 5  더불어민주당                 제주 제주시갑  constituency #> 6      새누리당                      비례대표  proportional #>                                                                                                        committees #> 1                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                        산업통상자원중소벤처기업위원회, 4차 산업혁명 특별위원회, 예산결산특별위원회, 교육문화체육관광위원회 #> 2                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                            #> 3                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                             농림축산식품해양수산위원회, 국회운영위원회, 보건복지위원회, 예산결산특별위원회 #> 4                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                농림축산식품해양수산위원회, 외교통일위원회, 정보위원회, 정치개혁 특별위원회, 행정안전위원회, 안전행정위원회 #> 5                                          과학기술정보방송통신위원회, 행정안전위원회, 예산결산특별위원회, 헌법개정 특별위원회, 외교통일위원회 #> 6 대법관(노태악) 임명동의에 관한 인사청문특별위원회, 법제사법위원회, 국회운영위원회, 공공부문 채용비리 의혹과 관련된 국정조사특별위원회, 환경노동위원회, 사법개혁특별위원회, 재난안전 대책 특별위원회, 청년미래 특별위원회, 과학기술정보방송통신위원회, 국무총리(이낙연) 임명동의에 관한 인사청문특별위원회, 헌법개정 특별위원회, 미래창조과학방송통신위원회 #>   gender birth_date seniority n_bills n_bills_lead #> 1      M 1942-06-05         4     312 what: Member-Level Roll Call Votes (22nd Assembly) — roll_calls
title: Member-Level Roll Call Votes (22nd Assembly) — roll_calls
text: Individual legislator voting records 1,286 bills went recorded plenary vote 22nd Korean National Assembly (2024-2026). row represents one legislator's vote one bill.

title: Member-Level Roll Call Votes (22nd Assembly) — roll_calls
code: roll_calls

title: Member-Level Roll Call Votes (22nd Assembly) — roll_calls
text: data frame 383,739 rows 8 variables: bill_id Bill identifier (links votes$bill_id bills$bill_id) assembly Assembly number (22) member_name Legislator name Korean member_id Legislator identifier (MONA_CD, links legislators$member_id) party Party affiliation time vote district Electoral district proportional list position vote Vote cast Korean: one four values meaning yes, , abstain, absent vote_date Date vote

title: Member-Level Roll Call Votes (22nd Assembly) — roll_calls
text: Open National Assembly Information API (Republic Korea), endpoint nojepdqqaweusdfbi.

title: Member-Level Roll Call Votes (22nd Assembly) — roll_calls
text: member-level roll call API available 22nd assembly. 20th 21st assemblies, use bill-level votes dataset. dataset enables ideal point estimation (e.g., W-NOMINATE), party unity scores, analysis legislative coalitions. Use member_id link legislators biographical metadata.

title: Member-Level Roll Call Votes (22nd Assembly) — roll_calls
code: data(roll_calls)  # Vote distribution table(roll_calls$vote) #>  #>   기권   반대   불참   찬성  #>   4886   6827  87877 284149   # Votes per party head(sort(table(roll_calls$party), decreasing = TRUE)) #>  #> 더불어민주당     국민의힘   조국혁신당       무소속       진보당     개혁신당  #>       212993       137372        15430         7043         4471         3858   # Number of unique legislators length(unique(roll_calls$member_id)) #> [1] 304

title: Run an interactive tutorial — run_tutorial
text: Launches learnr interactive tutorial browser. Students can type run code directly browser hints solutions. Requires learnr package.

title: Run an interactive tutorial — run_tutorial
code: run_tutorial(name)

title: Run an interactive tutorial — run_tutorial
text: name Tutorial name number (1-9). Use list_tutorials see available tutorials.

title: Run an interactive tutorial — run_tutorial
text: return value, called side effect launching learnr tutorial browser.

title: Run an interactive tutorial — run_tutorial
code: if (interactive()) {   run_tutorial(1) }

what: Policy Seminar Activity by Legislator-Year (2000-2025) — seminars
title: Policy Seminar Activity by Legislator-Year (2000-2025) — seminars
text: Annual panel policy seminar hosting activity legislators 16th 22nd Korean National Assembly. Policy seminars (jeongchaek semina) informal legislative events MPs invite experts, stakeholders, colleagues parties discuss policy issues.

title: Policy Seminar Activity by Legislator-Year (2000-2025) — seminars
code: seminars

title: Policy Seminar Activity by Legislator-Year (2000-2025) — seminars
text: data frame 5,962 rows 18 variables: name Legislator name Korean member_id Legislator identifier (MONA_CD, links legislators$member_id). Available ~95\ NA unmatched ambiguous (homonym) cases. year Calendar year assembly Assembly number (17-22) party Party affiliation camp Political camp: "liberal", "conservative", "progressive", "" (values Korean) seniority Number terms served n_seminars Number policy seminars hosted year n_cross_party Number seminars co-hosted -party legislators cross_party_ratio Share seminars cross-party (0-1) avg_coalition_size Average number co-hosts per seminar is_governing Logical: belongs governing (presidential) party is_female Logical: female legislator is_proportional Logical: proportional-representation member is_seoul Logical: represents Seoul district province Province/metro area electoral district total_terms Total assembly terms served across career n_bills_led Number bills proposed lead proposer year

title: Policy Seminar Activity by Legislator-Year (2000-2025) — seminars
text: National Assembly Seminar Database, collected via API.

title: Policy Seminar Activity by Legislator-Year (2000-2025) — seminars
text: Policy seminars distinctive feature Korean National Assembly. Unlike floor speeches committee hearings, seminars voluntary allow legislators signal policy expertise build cross-party ties. cross_party_ratio variable captures often legislator cooperates across party lines informal arena. is_governing variable enables difference--differences designs: party transitions opposition governing (vice versa), members' cross-party collaboration change?

title: Policy Seminar Activity by Legislator-Year (2000-2025) — seminars
code: data(seminars)  # Cross-party collaboration by governing status tapply(seminars$cross_party_ratio, seminars$is_governing, mean, na.rm = TRUE) #>     FALSE      TRUE  #> 0.3155702 0.2804488   # Seminar activity over time agg <- aggregate(n_seminars ~ year, data = seminars, FUN = sum) plot(agg, type = "b", main = "Total Policy Seminars by Year")   # Gender gap in seminar hosting tapply(seminars$n_seminars, seminars$is_female, median, na.rm = TRUE) #> FALSE  TRUE  #>     4     7

what: Set Korean font for ggplot2 — set_ko_font
title: Set Korean font for ggplot2 — set_ko_font
text: Detects Korean-compatible font current system applies ggplot2 plots via theme_set(). Call top script avoid broken Korean text plot titles labels.

title: Set Korean font for ggplot2 — set_ko_font
code: set_ko_font(font = NULL)

title: Set Korean font for ggplot2 — set_ko_font
text: font Optional font family name use directly. NULL (default), auto-detects common Korean fonts.

title: Set Korean font for ggplot2 — set_ko_font
text: font family name used (invisibly).

title: Set Korean font for ggplot2 — set_ko_font
code: if (interactive()) {   library(ggplot2)   set_ko_font()    # Now Korean text renders correctly   ggplot(data.frame(x = 1), aes(x, x)) +     geom_point() +     labs(title = "Korean Title Test") }

what: Committee Speeches from the Science and ICT Committee (22nd Assembly) — speeches
title: Committee Speeches from the Science and ICT Committee (22nd Assembly) — speeches
text: Full corpus 15,843 speech records Science, Technology, Information, Broadcasting Communications Committee 22nd Korean National Assembly (2024). Standing committee meetings .

title: Committee Speeches from the Science and ICT Committee (22nd Assembly) — speeches
code: speeches

title: Committee Speeches from the Science and ICT Committee (22nd Assembly) — speeches
text: data frame 15,843 rows 9 variables: assembly Assembly number (22) date Date committee meeting committee Committee name Korean speaker Speaker label appears minutes (may include titles) role Speaker role: "legislator", "chair", "minister", "vice_minister", "senior_bureaucrat", "agency_head", "witness", "expert_witness", "nominee", "minister_nominee", "testifier", "public_corp_head", "broadcasting", "committee_staff" speaker_name Cleaned speaker name titles removed member_id Legislator identifier (MONA_CD, links legislators$member_id). Available rows; however, non-legislator speakers (ministers, witnesses, etc.) match entries legislators. speech_order Order speech turn within meeting speech Full text speech Korean

title: Committee Speeches from the Science and ICT Committee (22nd Assembly) — speeches
text: National Assembly committee minutes via Open National Assembly Information API.

title: Committee Speeches from the Science and ICT Committee (22nd Assembly) — speeches
text: dataset contains complete standing committee speech records (sampling) Science ICT Committee 22nd assembly (June-December 2024). Speeches shorter 50 characters excluded. role variable distinguishes legislators government officials, witnesses, participants. Filter role == "legislator" MP speeches , compare legislators ministers discuss agenda items. committee covers AI, telecommunications, broadcasting, space policy, R&D governance, making suitable keyword analysis, topic modeling, text analysis exercises.

title: Committee Speeches from the Science and ICT Committee (22nd Assembly) — speeches
code: data(speeches)  # Distribution of speech lengths hist(nchar(speeches$speech), breaks = 100,      main = "Speech Length Distribution", xlab = "Characters")   # Speaker roles table(speeches$role) #>  #>       agency_head      broadcasting             chair   committee_staff  #>                31                46              3821                27  #>    expert_witness        legislator          minister  minister_nominee  #>               720              9098               196               151  #>           nominee  public_corp_head senior_bureaucrat         testifier  #>               349               187                62                77  #>     vice_minister           witness  #>                70              1008   # Most frequent legislator speakers leg <- speeches[speeches$role == "legislator", ] head(sort(table(leg$speaker_name), decreasing = TRUE), 10) #>  #>   김현 노종면 최형두 이훈기 이정헌 한민수 황정아 김우영 조인철 이준석  #>   1105    826    717    630    576    527    512    503    491    384   # Simple keyword search (example: AI-related speeches) ai <- speeches[grepl("AI", speeches$speech), ] nrow(ai) #> [1] 294

what: Plenary Vote Results in the Korean National Assembly (20th-22nd) — votes
title: Plenary Vote Results in the Korean National Assembly (20th-22nd) — votes
text: Bill-level vote tallies plenary sessions 20th 22nd Korean National Assembly (2016-2026). row represents one bill went recorded floor vote.

title: Plenary Vote Results in the Korean National Assembly (20th-22nd) — votes
code: votes

title: Plenary Vote Results in the Korean National Assembly (20th-22nd) — votes
text: data frame 8,050 rows 13 variables: bill_id Unique bill identifier (links bills$bill_id) bill_no Numeric bill number bill_name Full bill title Korean assembly Assembly number (20, 21, 22) committee Standing committee bill referred vote_date Date plenary vote result Vote outcome Korean (e.g., passed -, passed amendments, rejected) bill_type Type bill (e.g., legislation, budget, resolution) total_members Total number assembly members time voted Number members cast vote yes Number yes votes Number votes abstain Number abstentions

title: Plenary Vote Results in the Korean National Assembly (20th-22nd) — votes
text: Open National Assembly Information API (Republic Korea), endpoint ncocpgfiaoituanbr.

title: Plenary Vote Results in the Korean National Assembly (20th-22nd) — votes
text: bills go floor vote. bills disposed committee expire end assembly term. votes dataset captures reached plenary floor recorded vote. 40\ bills contains legislator-proposed bills votes also includes committee alternatives, budget bills, resolutions separate identifiers. See roll_calls member-level voting records (22nd assembly), useful ideal point estimation party discipline analysis.

title: Plenary Vote Results in the Korean National Assembly (20th-22nd) — votes
code: data(votes)  # Votes per assembly table(votes$assembly) #>  #>   20   21   22  #> 3492 3272 1286   # Pass rate table(votes$result) #>  #>     부결 수정가결 원안가결  #>       26     2688     5336   # Average yes rate votes$yes_rate <- votes$yes / votes$voted summary(votes$yes_rate) #>     Min.  1st Qu.   Median     Mean  3rd Qu.     Max.  #> 0.006369 0.961690 0.983784 0.961513 0.994143 1.000000   # Contentious votes (yes rate < 70%) contentious <- votes[votes$yes / votes$voted < 0.7, ] nrow(contentious) #> [1] 144

title: Legislator Asset Declarations (2015-2025) — wealth
text: Panel data asset declarations 773 Korean National Assembly members across 13 reporting periods (2015-2025). Derived mandatory public disclosures via OpenWatch project.

title: Legislator Asset Declarations (2015-2025) — wealth
code: wealth

title: Legislator Asset Declarations (2015-2025) — wealth
text: data frame 2,928 rows 14 variables: member_id Legislator identifier (links legislators$member_id) year Disclosure year (2015-2025) name Legislator name Korean total_assets Total declared assets, thousands KRW total_debt Total declared liabilities, thousands KRW net_worth Net worth (assets minus debt), thousands KRW real_estate Total real estate value, thousands KRW building Total building/structure value, thousands KRW land Total land value, thousands KRW deposits Total bank deposits, thousands KRW stocks Total stock holdings, thousands KRW n_properties Total number properties disclosed has_seoul_property Logical: owns property Seoul has_gangnam_property Logical: owns property Gangnam (Seoul's wealthiest district)

title: Legislator Asset Declarations (2015-2025) — wealth
text: OpenWatch (https://docs.openwatch.kr/data/national-assembly), CC -SA 4.0 license.

title: Legislator Asset Declarations (2015-2025) — wealth
text: monetary values thousands KRW (1 unit = 1,000 won). convert billions won, divide 1,000,000. example, net_worth 1,670,000 means 1.67 billion won (approximately USD 1.2 million). Legislators required law disclose assets annually. legislators appear every year, panel unbalanced (entries correspond active service periods).

title: Legislator Asset Declarations (2015-2025) — wealth
code: data(wealth)  # Distribution of net worth (in billions of won) hist(wealth$net_worth / 1e6, breaks = 50,      main = "Legislator Net Worth", xlab = "Billion KRW")   # Real estate as share of total assets wealth$re_share <- wealth$real_estate / wealth$total_assets summary(wealth$re_share) #>    Min. 1st Qu.  Median    Mean 3rd Qu.    Max.  #>  0.0000  0.4286  0.5895  0.5750  0.7282  0.9872   # Gangnam property owners vs others tapply(wealth$net_worth / 1e6, wealth$has_gangnam_property, median, na.rm = TRUE) #>    FALSE     TRUE  #> 1.144683 2.952746

title: assemblykor 0.1.2
text: Fixed donttest example failure reported CRAN ‘Additional issues’. remote parquet files served get_bill_texts() get_proposers() re-encoded ZSTD GZIP compression CRAN’s default arrow build (include ZSTD codec) can read without error. Examples get_bill_texts() get_proposers() now write cache file tempdir() rather user cache directory, R CMD check runs longer leave files behind.

title: assemblykor 0.1.1
text: CRAN release: 2026-04-07 get_bill_texts(), get_proposers(): changed \donttest{} (download functions). open_tutorial(), run_tutorial(), set_ko_font(): changed (interactive()) {} (interactive system-dependent functions).

title: assemblykor 0.1.0
text: Initial CRAN release. Seven built-datasets: legislators, bills, wealth, seminars, speeches, votes, roll_calls. Two download functions larger datasets: get_bill_texts(), get_proposers(). Nine Korean-language interactive tutorials (learnr) plain R Markdown versions covering tidyverse, visualization, regression, panel data, text analysis, network analysis, roll call analysis, bill success prediction, speech pattern analysis. Utility functions: set_ko_font(), path_to_file(), list_tutorials(), open_tutorial(), run_tutorial().
